prompt: A video of a ripe yellow banana lying on a table. At the start it rests on one side of the table. A strong invisible push immediately sends it sliding towards the right, and it keeps moving for the entire clip — continuously gliding towards the right across the table like a small box shoved hard across a rough carpet. Because the surface has high friction, it slides only a short but clearly visible distance — it noticeably moves away from its starting spot — before friction fairly quickly stops it. Throughout the video the banana is always in motion, never frozen, visibly travelling across the frame and ending up away from where it began. Everything else — the table and every other object in the scene — stays perfectly still, and the camera does not move.

A video of a ripe yellow banana lying on a table. At the start it rests on one side of the table. A strong invisible push immediately sends it sliding towards the right, and it keeps moving for the entire clip — continuously gliding towards the right across the table like a hockey puck gliding across fresh, slippery ice. Because the surface is extremely slick with almost no friction, it slides a long way and keeps going, travelling most of the way across the frame before only very slowly easing to a stop. Throughout the video the banana is always in motion, never frozen, visibly travelling across the frame and ending up away from where it began. Everything else — the table and every other object in the scene — stays perfectly still, and the camera does not move.

A video of a green inflatable bean bag chair skidding across a wooden office floor. It starts at the left, resting on the wood floor with open floor to its right. Off-screen, someone gives it a hard shove to the right, and the lightweight air-filled chair immediately skids and slides rightward across the slick wooden floor, scooting across the open floor and staying in motion the whole time. Because the wood floor grips a bit, the bean bag chair skids only a short but clearly obvious distance to the right — it plainly leaves its starting spot — before friction fairly quickly stops it. By the end of the clip the chair has clearly travelled to the right, leaving the spot where it started empty. Only the bean bag chair moves; the Canon printer, blue recycling bin, gray carpet and walls stay completely fixed, and the camera is locked off and does not move.
